tested.name: Category-slot detachability (is a concept's category a movable coordinate?)

concept.name: Category-slot detachability (is a concept's category a movable coordinate?)
concept.definition: For an entity concept E (e.g. apple) with taxonomic category C (fruit), the category is a SEPARABLE coordinate iff a steering intervention can swap C->C' (vegetable) while preserving E's non-category attributes (red, round, sweet, grows-on-tree). Operationalize on a small open model (gpt2-small): dir(C)=diff-in-means (fruit prompts minus grand mean) at layer L; dir(C')=diff-in-means (vegetable). Intervene on the last token of category-eliciting prompts about E ('An apple is a kind of ___', 'The category of apple is ___') by adding alpha*dir(C') and subtracting alpha*dir(C). Sweep alpha and L. Measure three numbers per (alpha,L): (a) FLIP = P(model classifies E as C') via next-token / completion; (b) PRESERVE = E's non-category attribute score (red/round/sweet still completed/probed high); (c) COHERENCE = perplexity within tol of clean.
concept.prediction: There EXISTS an (alpha,L) window where FLIP>=0.7 AND PRESERVE>=0.7 AND COHERENCE within 20% of clean baseline — a CLEAN reclassification (apple becomes 'a vegetable' yet stays red/round/sweet). This means taxonomic category is a detachable coordinate, not fused with identity. FALSIFIED if no window achieves FLIP and PRESERVE jointly (only gibberish, i.e. coherence collapses whenever FLIP rises, or FLIP never reaches 0.7). Controls: random unit vector of equal norm must NOT flip category (else we merely broke the model with noise); positive control alpha*dir(C) on E must intensify correct category (oracle).
concept.reduces_to: Activation steering / ActAdd (Turner et al. 2023) + hierarchical concept geometry (Park et al. 2024). Risk: 'steering works' restated. Earns keep ONLY by measuring clean DETACHMENT (category moves while identity attributes are preserved and coherence holds), which neither prior line tests.

test.design:
Preregistered falsification (PREREG.md + exp.py + RESULT.txt in research_cycle/experiments/category_slot/). Model gpt2, cpu, ONE intervention: last-token residual add of a category axis v = mean_resid(veg exemplars) - mean_resid(fruit exemplars), exemplars decoupled from the target (fruits banana/orange/grape/peach/mango/pear vs vegetables carrot/potato/broccoli/onion/celery/spinach). Target = "apple". Grid L in {6,8,10} x alpha in {2,4,6,8}. Metrics on apple: FLIP = fraction of 6 category probes where clean prefers ' fruit' (logitdiff<0) and steered flips to ' vegetable' (logitdiff>0); PRESERVE = fraction of 5 identity probes whose argmax-within-candidate-set (red/round/sweet/juicy/tree) stays == clean; COHERENCE = mean next-token entropy on 6 neutral non-food prompts under the same hook, within 20% band 0.8-1.2 of clean. Frozen decision rule: BROKEN if oracle<1.0 logit; SUPPORTED if some (L,alpha) hits FLIP>=0.7 AND PRESERVE>=0.7 AND coherence-in-band AND random-control FLIP<0.5 AND oracle passes; REFUTED if oracle passes but no config meets FLIP&PRESERVE jointly in band.</design>
<parameter name="numbers">Clean sanity: apple category logitdiff mean -1.11 (5/6 probes prefer 'fruit') so a flip is meaningful. Winning windows: L=8 a=6 -> FLIP 0.83, PRES 0.80, cohR 0.99; L=10 a=2 -> FLIP 0.83, PRES 1.00, cohR 1.00 (cleanest). FLIP ceiling is 5/6=0.83 because one probe is already veg-leaning at baseline. PRESERVE is a live metric: at L=10 a=8 it drops to 0.60 (overdrive breaks identity). Coherence essentially untouched everywhere (cohR 0.99-1.05).
test.sanity: Oracle PASSES strongly: adding +veg-direction to REAL vegetables' category probes intensifies the correct category by up to +5.06 logit (already +0.97..+2.74 at moderate alpha), well above min_oracle 1.0 — the measurement pipeline works (guards the .norm-bug class). Random control PASSES: a gaussian unit vector of the SAME norm at the winning L=8/a=6 gives FLIP 0.00 (PRES 0.80, cohR 0.92) — the flip is direction-specific, not noise breaking the model.

tested.name: Causal hierarchy containment (does ablating the parent knock out membership but spare identity?)

concept.name: Causal hierarchy containment (does ablating the parent knock out membership but spare identity?)
concept.definition: If child concept E (apple) is stored as parent-category component (fruit) PLUS a child-specific residual, then projecting the parent direction OUT of the residual stream should remove E's parent-membership while preserving E's identity attributes. Operationalize (gpt2-small): dir(fruit)=diff-in-means; on apple prompts, ablate by projecting dir(fruit) out at layer L (resid -= (resid.u)u). Measure MEMBERSHIP = drop in 'apple is a fruit' logit/completion, and IDENTITY = change in 'apple is red / round / grows on a tree' scores.
concept.prediction: Fruit-ablation drops MEMBERSHIP by a large margin while IDENTITY drops far less: specifically membership-drop >= 3x identity-drop, and identity stays above 0.6 of clean. This is causal evidence apple is represented as fruit-component + independent identity. FALSIFIED if identity falls as much as membership (concept is monolithic, category not separable) or if membership barely moves (ablation ineffective -> BROKEN_MEASUREMENT, check oracle: ablating dir(fruit) must reduce fruit-classification of a KNOWN fruit).
concept.reduces_to: Activation patching / ablation + hierarchical geometry (Park et al.). Earns keep as a CAUSAL containment test (ablate parent, watch child membership vs identity dissociate) rather than the correlational 'child vector lies near parent subspace' geometry claim.

test.design: Ran a preregistered ablation test on gpt2 (CPU). ONE intervention: project the `fruit` direction — diff-in-means of fruit vs vegetable exemplars, DECOUPLED from the target apple — out of resid_post at all positions of layer L (rank-1 projection, no magnitude knob). Metrics on apple only: MEMBERSHIP = fruit-vs-veg probability P(fruit)/(P(fruit)+P(veg)) over 6 "apple is a type of" probes; IDENTITY = candidate-normalised retention of the model's own clean answers over 5 property probes (color/shape/taste/texture/grows-on). Frozen decision rule read at L* = argmax-oracle layer among {4,6,8,10} (layer chosen by ablation efficacy, NOT by result): SUPPORTED iff oracle>=1.0 logit AND membership_drop>=0.10 AND membership_drop>=3*identity_drop AND identity_retained>=0.6; REFUTED(monolithic) if identity falls comparably or retained<0.6; REFUTED(target-inert) if membership barely moves though oracle works; BROKEN_MEASUREMENT if oracle<1.0. Files: /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/hierarchy_containment/{PREREG.md,exp.py,RESULT.txt}.
test.numbers: At frozen L*=4: apple membership fruit-pref 0.718 -> 0.016 (membership_drop = +0.98, a 98% relative knockout); identity 0.658 -> 0.600 (identity_drop = +0.09, identity_retained = 0.91); coherence ratio 1.00. Rule check: 0.98 >= 3 x 0.09 (=0.27) and 0.91 >= 0.6 -> SUPPORTED. Pattern replicates at L=10 (md=+0.88, idd=-0.06, identity actually rises). At L6/L8 the axis is not linearly ablatable (oracle ~0), so no effect there.
test.sanity: Oracle (positive control, decoupled): the SAME ablation drops fruit-logit on KNOWN non-apple fruits (banana/orange/grape/peach) by +3.32 logit at L*=4 — far above min_oracle 1.0, so the measurement genuinely works (not a near-zero/.norm-bug failure). Second control: random rank-1 direction ablation at L4 over 5 seeds gives membership_drop mean +0.001 / max +0.035 vs the fruit-direction's +0.98 — the knockout is direction-specific, not generic layer damage. Clean baseline M0=0.72>0.5 confirms gpt2 knows apple=fruit, so the knockout is meaningful.
